stemHtml: During an APV instrument approach operation (a class A 3D approach), when the aircraft reaches the relevant minima, the pilot can only identify components of the approach lighting system. In this situation, he/she...

options.a: Can only continue the approach below the minima and complete the landing if the visual glide slope indicator is also identified.
options.b: Can continue the approach but can only land if a second set of visual references is identified before 100 ft AGL.
options.c: CANNOT continue below the minima and the approach must be discontinued.
options.d: Can continue the approach below the minima and can complete the landing.
